name: Umar ibn Sa'd
title: Deceived Commander
shortDescription: The commander of Kufa's army in Karbala who, out of greed for the governorship of Rey, agreed to fight Imam Hussain (AS) and shot the first arrow.
fullStory:
Umar ibn Sa'd was the son of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (a famous commander in early Islam). He was supposed to go with an army to 'Rey' to suppress rebels and become its governor, but Ibn Ziyad ordered him to first go to Karbala and get allegiance from Hussain (AS).

Umar Sa'd initially didn't want to fight the Imam and tried to resolve the issue through negotiation, but when Ibn Ziyad threatened to take away the governorship of Rey and destroy his house, worldly greed overcame him.

He took command of the 30,000-strong army in Karbala. On the morning of Ashura, to show off to Ibn Ziyad, he shot the first arrow towards the Imam's tents and shouted: 'Bear witness for me before the Emir that I shot the first arrow.'

Umar Sa'd ordered horses to be trampled over the Imam's body and the tents to be set on fire. He never reached the governorship of Rey and was eventually killed disgracefully in his bed by Mukhtar al-Thaqafi.
quoteArabic: اِشْهَدُوا لِي عِنْدَ الأَمِيرِ أَنِّي أَوَّلُ مَنْ رَمى...
quote: (Upon shooting the first arrow:) Bear witness for me before the Emir (Ibn Ziyad) that I was the first to shoot.
